reasoning: Google Year in Search 'Trending' lists rank individuals based on the spike in their search volume compared to the previous year. Because Joe Biden was the US President and ran for re-election for parts of 2024, his baseline search volume in 2024 was astronomically high. After leaving office in early 2025, his search volume will almost certainly drop significantly. This means his 'trending' score (which measures year-over-year increases) will be negative or nonexistent, making it virtually impossible for him to even make the 2025 Trending People list, let alone rank exactly #2 globally. Combined with the very low base rate of predicting any exact slot for a specific person, the probability is pushed to the minimum allowed bound of 0.05.
